title: Playbook - Microsoft Graph SharePoint List And List Item CRUD
applies_to: Create, read, update, and delete SharePoint lists and list items through Microsoft Graph
prerequisites: Microsoft Entra app with Graph token acquisition working
SharePoint site hostname and site-relative path, site ID, or site-discovery strategy
Permissions aligned to list creation or list item access requirements
required_permissions: List creation: least privileged delegated or application permission is Sites.Manage.All
List item read and write patterns typically require Sites.Read.All or Sites.ReadWrite.All depending on operation
For unattended automation, use application permissions with admin consent where required
request_sequence: Resolve the target site by path, search, tenant enumeration, or direct site ID.
List available lists if needed: GET /sites/{site-id}/lists.
Create a new list when required: POST /sites/{site-id}/lists with displayName, list template, and optional columns.
Create an item: POST /sites/{site-id}/lists/{list-id}/items with a body containing fields.
Update an item: PATCH /sites/{site-id}/lists/{list-id}/items/{item-id}/fields with changed columns only.

confirmed_facts.fact: Lists can be enumerated from a site with GET /sites/{site-id}/lists.
List creation uses POST /sites/{site-id}/lists, and if the list facet or template is unspecified the list defaults to the genericList template, which includes a Title column.
List items can be listed and expanded with fields through the list item APIs.
List item creation uses POST to the list items collection, with column values inside the fields object.
List item updates are performed against the item fields resource with PATCH semantics.
List item deletion uses DELETE /sites/{site-id}/lists/{list-id}/items/{item-id} and returns 204 No Content on success.

implementation_notes: Expand fields when reading items so column values arrive in one pass.
Treat internal field names as the write contract, not display labels.
Create the list only once and then cache site ID and list ID for repeat operations.
If concurrency matters, use the if-match header with item deletion or updates to avoid accidental overwrites or deletes on stale data.
common_failures: 403 when the token lacks the required Sites scopes for list creation or item writes.
400 when the request uses display names instead of internal field names.
404 when the resolved site ID or list ID does not belong to the intended tenant site.
412 Precondition Failed when using if-match with a stale eTag.
